title: Oregon State App #1 — First Draft
html:
January 2021 first-draft pair of Oregon State application essays — the "obstacle" essay (Yahir's father's alcoholism and his own arc from numbness to forgiveness) and the "personal growth" essay (winning the Young Entrepreneurs Business Week Nick Fisher Award by uniting a diverse student company). First time the Washington-as-leader frame appears in his college writing — "neither the strongest soldier nor the brightest man, but he surrounded himself with talented people."

TL;DR

  • Drafted as part of Yahir's 2021 scholarship essay machine; this same pair of stories (alcoholism arc + YEBW Nick Fisher Award) gets recycled across nearly every scholarship app in this slice.
  • The Washington / Washington: The Indispensable Man (James Flexner biography) is cited as the leadership model — leverage talented people, don't be the strongest in the room.
  • The "game day at YEBW" anecdote is the load-bearing concrete moment: sacrificed an afternoon of work to play games, broke incompatibility, won 2nd place + Nick Fisher Award.
  • The forgiveness reframe — "choosing forgiveness does not mean forgetting, but choosing reconciliation" — is the rhetorical hinge that recurs in Al Forthan speech and the Al Forthan essays.
  • Earliest articulation in this slice of the "catalyst that brings collaboration" self-concept — Yahir as the connective tissue between mismatched people.

osu · yebw · george washington · alcoholism in the family · scholarship essay cycle · chosen one frame
